member.name: Dr Marie Tidball

addresses: 1B Lound Side, Chapeltown, Sheffield, South Yorkshire, S35 2UQ

member.synopsis: Dr Marie Tidball is the Labour MP for Penistone and Stocksbridge , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: School Meals: Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of ingredient costs on local authority school catering services; and what steps her Department is taking to help support local authorities to work
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises the pressures that some schools and suppliers may be facing due to rising costs. We are working closely with the school food sector to understand the wider school food system to support the delivery of the free school meals expan...

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit: Disability Premium
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what plans she has to roll out backdated payments for enhanced disability premium for claimants who transferred to Universal Credit before 14 February 2024; and whether these payments will be ongoing fo
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are providing transitional protection to some customers who moved to Universal Credit following a change in their circumstances and not via the “managed migration” process.To end the losses new customers to Universal Credit were facing, new regulations...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bayer: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to ensure patients affected by Essure products receive financial compensation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Manufacturers can generally be held liable for any harm caused by a defective product under Part 1 of the Consumer Protection Act 1987, but claims must be brought within 10 years of the product being supplied.NHS Resolution manages clinical negligence and...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sodium Valproate: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to establish a redress scheme for people harmed by sodium valproate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is carefully considering the valuable work done by the Patient Safety Commissioner and the resulting Hughes Report, which set out options for redress for those harmed by valproate and pelvic mesh. This is a complex area of work, involving s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Family Proceedings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether she plans to publish a response to her Department's report entitled, Assessing risk of harm to children and parents in private law children, published in June 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice has published a full implementation plan and various updates to the report entitled “Assessing risk of harm to children and parents in private law children cases”, which is known as the Harm Panel report. The latest delivery update...

writtenQuestions.heading: Emergencies: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what recent progress his Department has made on strengthening national resilience.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As my Rt. Hon friend the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster set out in his statement in the House last week, the Government continues to make improvements to strengthen our national resilience.In April the UK Resilience Academy will be launched and will...

writtenQuestions.heading: Skin Diseases: Steroid Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has plans to support scientific evidence gathering for topical steroid withdrawal including (a) patient involvement in research (b) research into mechanisms underlying the condition, (c)
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department’s National Institute for Health Research (NIHR) welcomes funding applications for research into any aspect of human health, including topic steroid withdrawal. It is not usual practice to ring-fence funds for particular topics or conditions...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Skin Diseases: Steroid Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to speed up diagnosis times for patients with topical steroid withdrawal.
writtenQuestions.answerText: General practitioners are responsible for ensuring their own clinical knowledge, including of topical steroid withdrawal, remains up-to-date, and for identifying learning needs as part of their continuing professional development. This activity should inc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Skin Diseases: Steroid Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to take steps to help increase knowledge of topical steroid withdrawal for (a) GPs, (b) Dermatology departments and (c) medical schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has reviewed topical steroid withdrawal reactions, and first communicated about these reactions in September 2021 via our Drug Safety Update, which is read by a wide range of health care profe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help improve local bus services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Over the fourteen years the previous Government was in office thousands of vital bus services disappeared across the country, and local bus services were left in a shocking state.Improving local bus services is a key part of this government’s growth missi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Anti-social Behaviour: Urban Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to tackle antisocial behaviour on high streets and in town centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Town centres and high streets are the heart of our communities but for far too long rampant antisocial behaviour, shoplifting and street crime has been corroding people’s sense of safety in their communities.That’s why this Government will put thousands o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Winter Fuel Payment: Low Incomes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions , what steps she is taking to help mitigate the potential impact of the withdrawal of Winter Fuel Payments on low income non-Pension Credit recipient pensioners.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Winter Fuel Payments will continue to be paid to pensioner households with someone receiving Pension Credit or certain other income-related benefits. They will continue to be worth £200 for eligible households, or £300 for eligible households with someone...

writtenQuestions.heading: Winter Fuel Payment: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps she is taking to help mitigate the potential impact of the withdrawal of Winter Fuel Payments on disabled non-Pension Credit recipient pensioners.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Disability benefits provide a tax free, non-income-related contribution towards the extra costs people with a long-term health condition or disabilities can face, such as additional heating costs. Attendance Allowance can be worth up to £5,600 a year. Ent...

writtenQuestions.heading: Inland Waterways: Pollution
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent progress he has made on banning the payment of bonuses to the executives of water companies that are found to have polluted waterways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The opposition had 14 years to legislate to ban the bonuses of water bosses – and they didn’t. This Labour Government has done it in less than 70 days. Through our Water (Special Measures) Bill, water companies will be put under tough special measures. Th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
